topic_title: Edge cases — testing what happens at the boundary of expected input

required_sections.detail: Sections 3 (Learning Objectives, 5 bullet points, ~70 words), 4 (Introduction, 3 paragraphs, ~170 words), 5 (Core Concepts, 5 subsections, ~1100 words), and 10 (Key Takeaways, 5 bullet points, ~100 words) are all present and non-trivial. All meet the minimum word-count thresholds (§5 >=100 words; §3/§4/§10 >=50 words). No placeholder strings in required sections.

citation_density.detail: Strict mode (resources_source=web_search). Primary resource [1] (edbennett.github.io Carpentries Python testing lesson) cited in §4 and extensively throughout §§5, 6, 7, 8, 10. Supporting resources [2] (runestone.academy) and [3] (freecodecamp.org) each cited in multiple sections. Estimated citation coverage in §§5, 6, 7 is approximately 88% of standalone factual assertions — well above the 60% threshold. All citation markers [1], [2], [3] resolve to named resources in topic.resources.json. No fabricated or unresolvable markers found.

sequence_drift.detail: All technical terms are either: (a) introduced in this corpus (edge case, boundary value, equivalence class, off-by-one error, test prediction, minimum/maximum valid input, empty input), (b) established in prior topics 11.1–11.8 (falsy from 11.4; if/else and comparison operators from 11.5; list, for loop, accumulator pattern from 11.6; specification, spec-first discipline from 11.7; silent failure, loud failure from 11.8), or (c) named-and-deferred in ≤1 sentence with an explicit covered-later pointer (pytest, unittest in §7: 'a topic for later in your learning'; regression check in §7: 'a formal testing concept you will cover later'). No undeferred forward references detected.

length_sanity.detail: Estimated word count ~2,900 words after cycle-2 scope_creep removals (confirmation-bias paragraph ~100w removed from a ~3,000w corpus). This is approximately 100 words (3.3%) below the standard-profile floor of 3,000. The hard-fail threshold is 'more than 30% outside the range' (floor = 2,100w); ~2,900w does not trigger this. Soft note: corpus is at the low end of the standard range. Author may optionally add ~100w of body content to reach the floor, but this is not blocking passage.

findings.detail: FIXED from cycle 2. Bold formatting removed; the defining appositive clause has been replaced with a plain-text named-and-deferred mention: 'Professionals call this a regression check (a formal testing concept you will cover later)'. This is ≤1 sentence with an explicit deferral pointer — allowed per scope_discipline rules. No scope_creep violation.

FIXED from cycle 2. The ~100-word paragraph teaching confirmation bias as a cognitive-psychology concept has been fully removed. The term 'confirmation bias' does not appear anywhere in the corpus. Section 5.5 now focuses solely on the test-prediction technique, which is within scope.

No new scope_creep findings. pytest/unittest (§7) are named-and-deferred in one sentence. equivalence class is directly relevant to understanding edge-case boundaries and defined at appropriate beginner depth.

Soft finding carried from cycle 2 (not resolved but also not escalated): 'A4 portfolio assessment (due week 13)' in §4 uses 'A4' without a plain-language gloss of the label itself. The phrase 'portfolio assessment' is descriptive, and the due-week parenthetical provides context. This single soft finding is well below the 3-finding hard-escalation threshold.

beginner_readability.detail: 1 soft finding total (down from 2 in cycle 2; the confirmation-bias undefined-term finding was resolved by scope_creep fix). Threshold for hard escalation is 3 distinct findings. No jargon dumps. Sentence length consistently under 25 words. Register is plain and analogy-driven. No hedging phrases. Soft pass.

verdict: PASS. Both cycle-2 hard-fail scope_creep items are confirmed fixed: (1) 'regression check' bold and defining clause removed — now a plain-text named-and-deferred one-liner with explicit covered-later pointer (§7); (2) confirmation-bias paragraph fully removed — term does not appear in corpus. All 6 checks pass. Soft note on length: estimated ~2,900w is ~100w (3.3%) below the standard-profile floor of 3,000w after the paragraph removal, but the hard-fail threshold (>30% outside range) is not triggered. Citation density ~88%, well above the 60% strict-mode threshold. No forward-reference violations. Beginner readability soft-passes with 1 minor soft finding (A4 label), below the 3-finding hard-escalation threshold. Corpus is approved to proceed to artifact fan-out (reading, quiz, diagram).
